checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's structure and text content. The repetition of the 'Professional Services' section (headings 7 and 8) in the JSON reflects the content found on pages 11 and 12 of the source PDF, where the text is partially repeated or continued. There is no evidence of invented text, table of contents, or other hallucinations.
The URL present in the Symantec training section (page 3 of the PDF) is correctly captured in the JSON. No other contact information (phone, email, URLs) appears to have been fabricated.

Several whole pages of content are missing from the conversion. Specifically, Page 13 of the source PDF (Lines 30-43 of the Carahsoft Trellix Price List) is entirely absent from both the extracted tables and the images. Additionally, Page 15 of the source PDF (the continuation of the DLT CrowdStrike Price List, containing the summary table and 'Notes') is missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 13: 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' containing line numbers 30 through 43. Page 15: 'DLT (CrowdStrike) Price List (Continued)' containing the 'Total - Pricing/Year' table and 'Notes' section.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON skips from the truncated table on page 12 directly to the CrowdStrike section on page 14. It then skips from page 14 directly to the SHI CrowdStrike section on page 16.
checks.b_evidence_location: Trellix and CrowdStrike sections, indices following heading-9 and heading-11.

checks.a_analysis: Several tables in the output are severely truncated, retaining only the first one or two rows of data. 1) The 'Carahsoft Trellix Price List - Lines 10 to 29' table only contains lines 10 and 11, missing 18 out of 20 rows. 2) The 'Cost Proposal Form - Costs Items' for DLT CrowdStrike (page 14) only contains the first row, missing 8 out of 9 rows. 3) The 'SHI Cost Items Pricing' table (page 16) only contains the first row, missing at least 7 rows. 4) The 'Presidio Cisco Pricing' table (page 17) only contains the first row, missing 9 out of 10 rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source Page 12 (Lines 10-29), Page 14 (DLT Costs Items), Page 16 (SHI Cost Items), and Page 17 (Presidio Cost Items) all contain tables with 8 to 20 rows of data.
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding tables in the JSON output contain only 1 or 2 rows of data (e.g., 'Presidio Cisco Pricing' has 1 row in output vs 10 in source).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Due to the combined effect of missing pages (C3) and truncated tables (C4), the Trellix, CrowdStrike, and Presidio sections are missing over 75% of their intended informational content. For example, the Trellix price list stops after line 11 of 43, and the DLT CrowdStrike and Presidio Cisco cost proposal forms are missing almost all their line items (Customization, Additional Modules, Documentation, Training, etc.).
checks.b_evidence_source: Complete cost breakdown tables for Trellix, CrowdStrike, and Presidio across pages 12-17.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON sections provide only a fractional represention of these lists.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for Trellix, CrowdStrike, and Presidio.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output contains minor OCR errors and formatting glitches (like the repeated Professional Services section), but the primary failures are the massive omissions noted in C3, C4, and C5.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains repeated sections under the 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' section, specifically the 'Professional Services' heading and its associated text and list (elements heading-7 and heading-8). However, a review of the source PDF (pages 11 and 12) shows that this entire section is repeated exactly in the original document. Per the audit instructions, intentional repetition in the source is not a defect.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON is consistent. It begins with an H2 (heading-1) followed by H3s for vendor titles and sub-sections. The 'Professional Services' sections within the Trellix portion correctly use H4 (heading-7, heading-8), nesting beneath the H3 vendor list. There are no skips in the hierarchy (e.g., H2 to H4).

The tables present in the JSON (Symantec, Professional Services, CrowdStrike, and Presidio) are structurally sound. Colspans are used correctly to represent the complex header structure of the Symantec list on page 3. The column counts match the source documents across the vendors where tables were extracted rather than imaged.

The JSON output is truncated. While it captures the first three vendors (Symantec, Sophos, and Trellix) across pages 3-15, it completely omits the sections for 'CROWDSTRIKE' (pages 16-18) and 'PRESIDIO (CISCO)' (page 19). The 'sections' array terminates prematurely, resulting in a significant loss of document structure and content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Vendor price lists for CrowdStrike (Pages 16-18) and Presidio (Cisco) (Page 19).
checks.b_evidence_output: The `sections` array ends after the 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' section (reading_order 3), omitting all subsequent vendor sections.

checks.a_analysis: The list in the Trellix section (pages 11-12) regarding the Security Engineer's responsibilities is correctly identified and structured as a list with four distinct items, matching the bullets in the source.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the number of headings in the JSON and the rendered HTML. The JSON contains 12 headings (including section titles and heading elements 1-9), while the rendered HTML contains 20. This difference of 8 headings exceeds the allowable threshold of 10% or 3+ headings. This mismatch corresponds to the missing sections for CrowdStrike and Presidio/Cisco identified in S4.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains approximately 20 distinct headings across all vendors.
checks.b_evidence_output: Headings in semantic.json: 12; Headings in rendered HTML: 20.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains a clear URL on page 3 (https://www.symantec.com/services/education-services/training-courses). In the JSON output, this URL is captured only as plain text within a table cell. The link count in the JSON metadata is 0, indicating that no clickable link structure was generated for this identifiable web address.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "... See Training Courses for each product here: https://www.symantec.com/services/education-services/training-courses" (link_count: 0)

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to represent the majority of the content found in the source PDF. Specifically, for the 'Attachment A. Cost Proposal Form' tables (found on PDF pages 14, 16, and 17 for CrowdStrike and Cisco), the HTML only renders the first row (Cloud Subscription/License Fee) and omits approximately 80% of the table rows, including sections for Customization, Additional Modules, Documentation, Installation, Training, and Maintenance. Additionally, the Carahsoft Trellix table (PDF pages 11-13) is truncated, showing only two rows (lines 10 and 11) out of 43. This is a massive omission of data that fundamentally changes the visual organization and utility of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Pages 14, 16, and 17 show comprehensive tables with multiple rows (Customization, Training, Maintenance, etc.). PDF Pages 11-13 show a 43-row product list.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML sections 5, 6, and 7 show tables with only 1 or 2 rows. Section 4 only shows line items 10 and 11.

checks.a_analysis: The tables that are rendered in the HTML (such as the Symantec and Cisco tables) maintain their visual structure with proper borders and headers. While the content is incomplete (see V1), the visual rendering of the existing table elements is correct and readable.

In the Sophos Price List section (corresponding to PDF pages 3-7), the HTML uses images to display the content. However, the images intended to represent PDF pages 4, 5, and 6 are 'broken' or missing, as they are populated with 1x1 transparent pixel data URLs. Only the images for pages 3 and 7 are valid. This results in the complete loss of visual data for three pages of the price list.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Pages 4, 5, and 6 contain detailed pricing tables for Sophos products.
checks.b_evidence_output: Three tags under the heading 'SHI (Sophos) Price List (Continued)' use a 1x1 transparent PNG data URL (data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJ...).

checks.a_analysis: There are multiple instances of content duplication. The main title 'ENDPOINT RESELLER PRICE LIST' is repeated as an H1 and then immediately again as an H2 within the first section. More significantly, the 'Professional Services' text block and its associated bullet points for the Trellix section are repeated twice verbatim (under Heading 7 and Heading 8).
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 9 contains one instance of the 'Professional Services' guidelines.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML contains two identical sections for 'Professional Services' (IDs 'heading-7' and 'heading-8').

checks.a_analysis: While the document uses clean CSS and includes accessibility features, the overall visual quality is severely degraded due to the massive loss of content described in V1 and V3. A government document missing over half its data rows and three entire pages of charts is functionally useless and visually incomplete.
checks.b_evidence_source: Entire 17-page PDF document containing numerous data tables.
checks.b_evidence_output: Short HTML document with fragmented tables and broken image placeholders.
